primary_cox

2026-04-30

library(tidyverse)
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.1     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.0     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.2.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(sas7bdat)

df <- read.csv("NHANES2.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# ----1. Clean data and create variables --------------------

#create a function to better cleaning the data
to_num <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(trimws(as.character(x))))


df_clean <- df %>%
  # death
  mutate(DEATH_clean = trimws(DEATH)) %>%
  filter(DEATH_clean %in% c("0", "1")) %>%
  mutate(DEATH_num = as.integer(DEATH_clean)) %>%
  
  # calculate age at exit for our time scale
  mutate(
    BORN_YR_n = to_num(BORN_YR),
    LAST_YR_n = to_num(LAST_YR),
    DIE_YR_n  = to_num(DIE_YR),
    age_exit  = if_else(
      DEATH_num == 1,
      (DIE_YR_n  + 1900) - (BORN_YR_n + 1900),
      (LAST_YR_n + 1900) - (BORN_YR_n + 1900)
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # exclude age_exit <= AGEYRS
  filter(age_exit > AGEYRS) %>%
  
  # BMI
  mutate(BMI = WT / (HEIGHT / 100)^2) %>%
  
  # NIACIN quartiles
  mutate(NIACIN_q = cut(
    NIACIN,
    breaks = c(-Inf, quantile(NIACIN, probs = c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75)), Inf),
    labels = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"),
    right  = TRUE
  )) %>%
  
  # Recode covariates
  mutate(
    Sex          = factor(SEX, levels = c(1, 2),
                          labels = c("Male", "Female")),
    Race         = factor(RACE, levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                          labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),
    Exercise     = factor(RECEX, levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                          labels = c("High", "Medium", "Low")),
    Diabetes     = factor(to_num(DIAB), levels = c(0, 1),
                          labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    Hypertension = factor(to_num(HTN_REP), levels = c(0, 1),
                          labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    Cholesterol  = to_num(SERCHOL),
    HDL          = to_num(HDL),
    Triglycerides= to_num(TRIGLYC),
    Death        = factor(DEATH_num, levels = c(0, 1),
                          labels = c("Alive", "Dead"))
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Age       = AGEYRS,
    Education = GRADES,
    Smoking   = AVGSMK,
    Alcohol   = BOOZE
  )

cat("Final analytic sample:", nrow(df_clean), "\n")
## Final analytic sample: 9241
cat("Deaths:", sum(df_clean$DEATH_num), "\n")
## Deaths: 2136
cat("Censored:", sum(df_clean$DEATH_num == 0), "\n")
## Censored: 7105
library(dplyr)
library(skimr)
library(tidyr)
library(survival)
## Warning: package 'survival' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(survminer)
## Loading required package: ggpubr
## 
## Attaching package: 'survminer'
## The following object is masked from 'package:survival':
## 
##     myeloma
library(haven)
library(broom)
## Warning: package 'broom' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(rms)
## Warning: package 'rms' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Loading required package: Hmisc
## Warning: package 'Hmisc' was built under R version 4.5.2
## 
## Attaching package: 'Hmisc'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     src, summarize
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     format.pval, units
library(stringr)
library(tableone)

# ----3. Create survival time variables --------------------

# follow-up time in years (for KM/log-rank only)
df_clean <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(fu_time = age_exit - Age)

# main Surv object for Cox models (age as time scale)
surv_age <- with(df_clean,
                 Surv(time = Age, time2 = age_exit, event = DEATH_num))

# Surv object for KM / log-rank (time since baseline)
surv_fu  <- with(df_clean,
                 Surv(time = fu_time, event = DEATH_num))

# ----4. Unadjusted Kaplan-Meier curves and log-rank test --------------------

# KM curves by niacin quartile (time since baseline)
fit_km <- survfit(surv_fu ~ NIACIN_q, data = df_clean)
ggsurvplot(fit_km, 
           data = df_clean, 
           risk.table = TRUE,
           risk.table.height = 0.25,
           risk.table.fontsize = 3,
           legend.title = "Niacin Quartile",
           legend.labs  = c("Q1 (lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4 (highest)"),
           xlim = c(0, 18),
           break.time.by = 5,
           ggtheme = theme_minimal(base_family = "sans", base_size = 11),
           tables.theme = theme_cleantable(base_family = "sans", base_size = 9))
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ggpubr package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/kassambara/ggpubr/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • colour : "Niacin Quartile"

# Log-rank test across niacin quartiles (unadjusted)
logrank <- survdiff(surv_fu ~ NIACIN_q, data = df_clean)
logrank
## Call:
## survdiff(formula = surv_fu ~ NIACIN_q, data = df_clean)
## 
##                N Observed Expected (O-E)^2/E (O-E)^2/V
## NIACIN_q=Q1 2314      581      526     5.744     7.761
## NIACIN_q=Q2 2311      551      529     0.883     1.196
## NIACIN_q=Q3 2308      524      535     0.230     0.313
## NIACIN_q=Q4 2308      480      545     7.863    10.758
## 
##  Chisq= 15  on 3 degrees of freedom, p= 0.002
# ----4. Primary Cox models (age-adjusted and multivariable) --------------------

# 4a. Age-adjusted Cox model (age as time scale, no covariates)
surv_obj <- with(df_clean,
                 Surv(time = Age, time2 = age_exit, event = DEATH_num)
                 )

cox_age <- coxph(
  surv_obj ~ NIACIN_q,
  data = df_clean
)
summary(cox_age)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = surv_obj ~ NIACIN_q, data = df_clean)
## 
##   n= 9241, number of events= 2136 
## 
##               coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)   
## NIACIN_qQ2 0.05550   1.05707  0.05951 0.933  0.35100   
## NIACIN_qQ3 0.10933   1.11553  0.06041 1.810  0.07033 . 
## NIACIN_qQ4 0.19045   1.20980  0.06217 3.063  0.00219 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##            exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## NIACIN_qQ2     1.057     0.9460    0.9407     1.188
## NIACIN_qQ3     1.116     0.8964    0.9910     1.256
## NIACIN_qQ4     1.210     0.8266    1.0710     1.367
## 
## Concordance= 0.514  (se = 0.007 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 10.07  on 3 df,   p=0.02
## Wald test            = 10.15  on 3 df,   p=0.02
## Score (logrank) test = 10.17  on 3 df,   p=0.02
# 4b. Multivariable Cox model (primary model)
cox_multiv <- coxph(
  surv_obj ~ NIACIN_q +
    Sex + Race + Education +
    Smoking + Alcohol + Exercise +
    BMI + Diabetes + Hypertension,
  data = df_clean,
  ties = "efron"
)
summary(cox_multiv)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = surv_obj ~ NIACIN_q + Sex + Race + Education + 
##     Smoking + Alcohol + Exercise + BMI + Diabetes + Hypertension, 
##     data = df_clean, ties = "efron")
## 
##   n= 9231, number of events= 2133 
##    (10 observations deleted due to missingness)
## 
##                      coef exp(coef)  se(coef)       z Pr(>|z|)    
## NIACIN_qQ2      -0.019324  0.980862  0.060614  -0.319 0.749878    
## NIACIN_qQ3      -0.023639  0.976638  0.063034  -0.375 0.707645    
## NIACIN_qQ4      -0.073540  0.929099  0.067273  -1.093 0.274329    
## SexFemale       -0.660632  0.516525  0.048478 -13.628  < 2e-16 ***
## RaceBlack       -0.123020  0.884246  0.073317  -1.678 0.093360 .  
## RaceOther       -0.367327  0.692583  0.198605  -1.850 0.064380 .  
## Education       -0.016248  0.983884  0.006117  -2.656 0.007899 ** 
## Smoking          0.020360  1.020569  0.001519  13.401  < 2e-16 ***
## Alcohol          0.007844  1.007875  0.004507   1.740 0.081780 .  
## ExerciseMedium   0.011642  1.011710  0.070182   0.166 0.868245    
## ExerciseLow      0.266097  1.304862  0.069684   3.819 0.000134 ***
## BMI             -0.022639  0.977615  0.004893  -4.627 3.71e-06 ***
## DiabetesYes      0.608511  1.837692  0.067931   8.958  < 2e-16 ***
## HypertensionYes  0.376544  1.457240  0.045625   8.253  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##                 exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## NIACIN_qQ2         0.9809     1.0195    0.8710    1.1046
## NIACIN_qQ3         0.9766     1.0239    0.8631    1.1051
## NIACIN_qQ4         0.9291     1.0763    0.8143    1.0600
## SexFemale          0.5165     1.9360    0.4697    0.5680
## RaceBlack          0.8842     1.1309    0.7659    1.0209
## RaceOther          0.6926     1.4439    0.4693    1.0222
## Education          0.9839     1.0164    0.9722    0.9957
## Smoking            1.0206     0.9798    1.0175    1.0236
## Alcohol            1.0079     0.9922    0.9990    1.0168
## ExerciseMedium     1.0117     0.9884    0.8817    1.1609
## ExerciseLow        1.3049     0.7664    1.1383    1.4958
## BMI                0.9776     1.0229    0.9683    0.9870
## DiabetesYes        1.8377     0.5442    1.6086    2.0994
## HypertensionYes    1.4572     0.6862    1.3326    1.5936
## 
## Concordance= 0.662  (se = 0.006 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 603.6  on 14 df,   p=<2e-16
## Wald test            = 665  on 14 df,   p=<2e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 688.6  on 14 df,   p=<2e-16
# ----5. Check nonlinearity of niacin (primary Cox model) --------------------

# Compare linear vs quartile niacin in multivariable Cox model
cox_linear <- coxph(
  surv_obj ~ NIACIN +
    Sex + Race + Education +
    Smoking + Alcohol + Exercise +
    BMI + Diabetes + Hypertension,
  data = df_clean
)

# Likelihood ratio test for nonlinearity
anova(cox_linear, cox_multiv, test = "LRT")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
##  Cox model: response is  surv_obj
##  Model 1: ~ NIACIN + Sex + Race + Education + Smoking + Alcohol + Exercise + BMI + Diabetes + Hypertension
##  Model 2: ~ NIACIN_q + Sex + Race + Education + Smoking + Alcohol + Exercise + BMI + Diabetes + Hypertension
##   loglik  Chisq Df Pr(>|Chi|)
## 1 -16224                     
## 2 -16223 1.2263  2     0.5416
# ----6. Check proportional hazards assumption --------------------

# Schoenfeld residual tests for NIACIN_q and global PH
ph_test <- cox.zph(cox_multiv)
ph_test
##                chisq df       p
## NIACIN_q      5.8128  3 0.12108
## Sex           1.3592  1 0.24367
## Race         14.7678  2 0.00062
## Education    11.8695  1 0.00057
## Smoking       0.0691  1 0.79262
## Alcohol       1.5403  1 0.21457
## Exercise     12.3311  2 0.00210
## BMI           2.4689  1 0.11612
## Diabetes     11.2922  1 0.00078
## Hypertension  4.0947  1 0.04302
## GLOBAL       46.1893 14 2.6e-05
ggcoxzph(ph_test[1])

# ----7. Create Table 2 --------------------

tidy_age <- tidy(cox_age, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE)
tidy_multiv <- tidy(cox_multiv, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE)
tidy_age_niacin <- tidy_age %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "^NIACIN_q"))

tidy_multiv_niacin <- tidy_multiv %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "^NIACIN_q"))

format_cox <- function(tidy_obj, model_label) {
  tidy_obj %>%
    mutate(
      NIACIN_q = str_replace(term, "NIACIN_q", ""),
      estimate = round(estimate, 2),
      conf.low = round(conf.low, 2),
      conf.high = round(conf.high, 2),
      HR_CI = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f, %.2f)", estimate, conf.low, conf.high),
      Model = model_label
    ) %>%
    select(Model, NIACIN_q, HR_CI)
}

tab_age <- format_cox(tidy_age_niacin, "Cox, age-adjusted")
tab_multiv <- format_cox(tidy_multiv_niacin, "Cox, multivariable")

# Add the reference row (Q1) to each table
ref_row <- tibble(
  Model = c("Cox, age-adjusted", "Cox, multivariable"),
  NIACIN_q = "Q1 (lowest)",
  HR_CI = "1.00 (ref)"
)

tab_cox <- bind_rows(
  ref_row,
  tab_age,
  tab_multiv
) %>%
  arrange(Model, NIACIN_q)

# Start with Cox only; Poisson columns are NA for now
table2_skeleton <- tab_cox %>%
  mutate(
    RR_Poisson_age = NA_character_,
    RR_Poisson_multiv = NA_character_
  )

library(flextable)
## 
## Attaching package: 'flextable'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:ggpubr':
## 
##     border, font, rotate
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     compose
library(officer)
## Warning: package 'officer' was built under R version 4.5.2
save_as_docx(
  table2_skeleton,
  path = "/Users/cutiepie/epi204/Table2_NHANES_NEJM.docx",
  pr_section = prop_section(
    page_size = page_size(orient = "landscape")
  )
)
